1 am glad to see that the Guels of

THE THOMOND HURLING SHIELD

c opening round of this year's Hurl-
ing Touenament for the splendid’ Shield
and valuable set of medals offered
promoters of the Thomond F
place at the Markets Field,
Sunday next. The contesting teams ‘will
be Limerick’ v. Tipperary and Clare v.
Cork. Hurling in Ireland hi
improved during recent year
has now become a science
ponents of this manly pastime have so
capably studied the art that every little
detail to arouse interest and enthusiasm
in the game has by now been mastered.
The manner in which the Thomond Feis
arrange matters and carry out the con
tests, it may here be remarked, is indeed
gratifying. They spare neither pains,
trouble nor energy in making the contests
worthy of the Gael, and worthy of the
aspirations of the great and illustrious
founder of the Gaelic Athletic Association,
Dr. Croke. The forthcoming Tourna.
‘ment will be the third organised by the
Promoters of the Thomond Feis. Cork
Secured the laurels last year, but it is
impossible to say who the winners will be
this year. as each team is putting on the
best and most tried men available: We
are, however, confident in assuring the
public that the forthcoming matches will
bein every respect well worth witnessing.

‘There's every chance that a Gaelic
football team will travel to San Francisco
for the big Gaelic meet to be held on the

Clare team and cause the players to bury
the hatchet. When McDonald was cap-
tain he had a strong team, but we noticed
that when he ‘lost the reins of office
things changed for the worse. But who-

Clare has no representation in the hurling
field here, due probably to a prejudiced
few who, unfortunately, were stuiccessful

hurling team. McDonald, it is rumoured,

WICKLOW,

As far back as October, last the Wick-
low County Board, at’ a meeting ia
Aughrim, started tO set their house in
order, and passed resolutions calculated
at the time to'counteract or remove abso- /
lutely the evil that bad been acti
injuriously to the well-being of the
in Wicklow.

Now that we are on the threshold, 30
to speak, of a new period, it is right that
a little examination of conscience on mat-
ters in general, including the result of
those resolutions mentioned in the open-
ing paragraph, should take place, and
though we do not intend to be even severe
a anyone, much less unjust, it is better
that plain tall should be the order of the -
day.

The County Convention, which was
held in Tinahely in May, induced most of
the delegates to believe that a successful
year awaited us, All the requirements
calculated to bring about such a result
Were present, sound financial, position,
plenty of clubs, and (apparently) officers
beyond criticism. Under such conditions
it was reasonable to anticipate that the
status of the G.A.A. in the county would
improve; that we would have 20 more
airing of petty grievances, no propagation,
of discord, but all should be united ia the
common cause. Well, itis hardly necese
sary to record that, instead of achieving
success, instead of Being in that prosper
us position which undoubtedly ‘awaited
us if anything like a decent, practical
effort had been made
are “dropping down the ladder rung by
rung.” ‘To the everlasting credit of
section of the powers that be, it must be
‘Said that they have done their duty. For
these we have nothing but praise. But
the others, who are really and truly’
merely hindrances to any cause or move-
ment they profess to serve—well, they are
beneath contempt. But they ‘won't mind
that. Such treatment will not effect any
improvement. They will just keep on at
